description: Marks the superessive case, indicating location on a surface with the implication of contact and support. Unlike mere position, 'nia' acknowledges the relationship between supporter and supported, the interface where distinct entities meet and interact.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'ni', where the nasal 'n' creates firm contact—the tongue pressed against the alveolar ridge mirrors the physical contact that 'on' describes. This grounding touch opens into 'a', the most open vowel, suggesting the broad expanse of surface that extends from the point of contact. The sound journey from firm touch to open space captures the dual nature of surfaces: specific in contact, expansive in extent. Speaking 'nia' requires the mouth to enact the very relationship it describes.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a pure locative preposition marking static position on surfaces. Precedes the noun it modifies: 'nia [table]' (on table = on the table), 'nia [wall]' (on wall = on the wall). Does not distinguish between horizontal and vertical surfaces—both involve surface contact. Cannot be used for abstract relationships that English 'on' permits (e.g., 'on Tuesday' requires temporal prepositions). Combines with motion verbs to indicate movement across surfaces rather than movement to end up on surfaces.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Regenerative design layers functions—solar collectors on roofs, gardens on buildings, living systems on structures. 'Nia' serves the practice of stacking functions where surfaces become productive interfaces rather than mere boundaries.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The preposition embodies the teaching of support without attachment—things can rest upon each other while maintaining their distinct nature. Like the lotus on water, 'nia' acknowledges contact without conflation, relationship without loss of identity.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional building and craft knowledge centers on understanding how things rest upon each other—stones on earth, beams on posts, roofs on walls. 'Nia' honors this fundamental relationship of support that creates shelter and stability without domination.
